title: Environment Variable Runtime Types (Priority Ordered, pruned)
description: Pruned ordered env value type definitions for deterministic value→type detection. Ambiguous types removed so detection falls back to primitives when ambiguous.

types.name: credentials/private_key_pem
types.description: PEM private key block. Normalize escaped/newline forms and validate with a PEM/crypto parser at runtime.

types.name: network/https_url
types.description: HTTPS-only URL (strict).

types.name: network/url
types.description: HTTP or HTTPS URL.

types.name: network/uri
types.description: Non-HTTP service URI (postgres://, redis://, s3://, mongodb+srv://). Explicitly excludes http/https.

types.name: network/domain
types.description: Domain/hostname with TLD.

types.name: network/ip
types.description: IPv4 dotted-decimal address.

types.name: network/ipv6
types.description: IPv6 address (common forms, compressed & full). For exhaustive validation use an IPv6 library.

types.name: version/semver
types.description: Semantic version (MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH) with optional pre-release/build metadata.

types.name: id/uuid
types.description: Canonical UUID (v1–v5) format.

types.name: structured/json
types.description: JSON-as-string pre-check. **Require** JSON.parse (or equivalent) success at runtime before accepting as structured JSON.

types.name: structured/key_value_pairs
types.description: Comma-separated key=value pairs (small maps). Loader should split and convert to object at runtime.

types.name: locale/langtag
types.description: Conservative BCP-47-like language tag (common cases). Prefer explicit enum for strict apps.

types.name: structured/email_list
types.description: Comma-separated list of emails (requires at least one comma). Loader should split & validate each email at runtime.

types.name: structured/csv
types.description: Comma-separated values stored as a single env string. Requires at least one comma to avoid single-token collisions.

types.name: file/windows_path
types.description: Windows absolute path (drive letter + backslashes). Place before POSIX file path detection so Windows values don't fall through.

types.name: file/path
types.description: POSIX-friendly file or directory path (relative or absolute). For Windows paths use file/windows_path.

types.name: temporal/cron
types.description: 5-field cron expression sanity check; validate with a cron parser at runtime.

types.name: temporal/time_hhmm
types.description: 24-hour time formatted HH:MM.

types.name: temporal/duration
types.description: Human-readable duration like '30s', '5m', '1h', '7d'.

types.name: credentials/secret
types.description: API key / token / secret. Avoid relying on regex only; runtime entropy checks or field-specific validators reduce false positives.

types.name: visual/hex_color
types.description: Hex color (#RGB, #RRGGBB, #RRGGBBAA). Low collision risk; place before final string fallback.

types.name: structured/enum
types.description: Enumerated value from a fixed set. Use [METHODS: pattern=^(opt1|opt2|...)$] to define valid options.

methods.pattern: new RegExp(pattern).test(value) // pattern should be ^(opt1|opt2|...)$ with valid options

types.name: float
types.description: Floating-point fallback (detect via parseFloat when reached).

methods.precision: limit decimal places (e.g. toFixed or check rounded equality)

types.name: integer
types.description: Generic integer fallback (detect via parseInt when reached).

types.name: boolean
types.description: Boolean fallback after coercion of common textual forms (true,false,1,0,yes,no).

types.name: string
types.description: Final fallback string.

methods.pattern: new RegExp(pattern).test(value)  // pattern should be a valid JS/PCRE regex string
